revision_summary: Three targeted fixes required: (1) ORPHANED_FLOW GAP — Stage 3 never evaluates the rep's failure to execute any orphaned_flow qualification steps after the existing-customer trigger fired at 00:20. Add two cherry_pick_moments: one for the orphaned_flow trigger (00:14–00:20) noting the rep skipped all four required orphaned_flow questions (own/lease, original company, panel size, system working), and one flagging the specific risk of transferring without confirming the system is working (hard DQ if broken). This is more precisely coachable than the CRM-prep framing and is directly schema-grounded. (2) MISCLASSIFICATION FIX — Reclassify cherry_pick_moments[3] ('I got about five minutes') from 'enthusiasm_signal' to 'time_constraint_protocol' and add the schema reference protocols.time_constraint. Revise the stewart_read to reflect that this is a boundary-setting statement consistent with the schema's time_constraint protocol, not an invitation signal. The coaching implication can retain the habit note about existing customers as warm surfaces, but should not frame the rep's transfer decision as a missed opportunity. (3) SCHEMA REFERENCE CORRECTION — Fix cherry_pick_moments[0] schema reference from context.lead_types_and_bill_collection_rules (inapplicable) to orphaned_flow. Reconcile the coaching_focus[0] counterfactual ('one minute of pre-call review would have changed the entire shape') with the cherry_pick_moments[0] acknowledgment that this may be a systems issue, not a rep issue — the coaching focus should present both branches conditionally rather than asserting the rep-prep framing as the primary read.
